campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different types of transportation.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing where residents could share their views on a proposed new rule or regulation. After listening to public input, the council approved moving forward with the ordinance and scheduled it to be officially adopted at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Final adoption scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their 2026 weed control efforts, establish official rules for how the council operates, and address affordable housing policies. The council also approved raising two flags celebrating Pride Month and Juneteenth, heard from the regional transit authority, and set their calendar for implementing new housing plans.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program across the city
New official procedures and norms for City Council meetings
Affordable housing unit transfer rules and exemptions
Flag raising celebrations for Progress Pride and Juneteenth
Housing Element implementation timeline and transit authority updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several construction and service contracts during this April 2026 meeting. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign agreements with various companies for construction work, professional services, and insurance coverage, as well as approved joining a statewide insurance program to help manage risk and costs.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority (CJPIA) for better insurance rates
Multiple contract amendments with existing service providers
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving young people and families. They discussed youth programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Empowerment Alliance, checked on code enforcement efforts, and looked at plans to improve library facilities and services across the city.
keyTopics: Youth programs and services for kids and teens in San José
Safe Summer Initiative and youth intervention services performance
Code enforcement activity across the city
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online. Residents could join virtually through Zoom or by phone to speak during public comment periods on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Instructions for raising hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they saluted the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their connections to Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official minutes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by commissioners about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The main topics included a budget and food program called 'Budget and Bites,' a youth-led organization called 'Unmuted Tabs,' and summer job opportunities for young people in the city.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for youth
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 11, 2026
Public comment period for residents on other city matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council is holding meetings in a hybrid format, meaning residents can attend either in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is set up to accept written public comments from residents before the meetings so people can weigh in on city issues.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and remote Zoom option)
Remote participation details: Zoom link 997-0675-9306 and phone dial-in available
Written public comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a tax board partnership with the county, deciding on fee waivers for a community education organization, and awarding a construction contract. The council also discussed housing and community development plans and reviewed new project ideas proposed by individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining county tax board partnership
Fee waiver decision for Milpitas Community Educational
Construction contract approval and project plans
Housing and community development plan updates
New council member project proposals

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's natural gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision was whether to approve changes to residential and commercial gas rates, along with moving money between different city funds to balance the gas utility budget.
keyTopics: Natural gas rate changes for homes and businesses
Gas utility budget and financial reserves for 2027
Transfer of funds between city accounts
Approval of meeting minutes from the Utilities Advisory Commission
